stem: A retail study measures customer satisfaction score across 4 chains, each containing multiple stores. chain A has 20 observations with mean 71.6 /10 (within-chain SD = 4.7). chain B has 8 observations with mean 48.8 /10 (within-chain SD = 3.7). chain C has 15 observations with mean 58.9 /10 (within-chain SD = 4.1). chain D has 8 observations with mean 59.4 /10 (within-chain SD = 7.5). The between-chain standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 8.4 and 21.0 /10. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true customer satisfaction score for chain C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-chain variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for chain C: 58.9 /10 (shrunk from raw 58.9). Range [58.9, 59.0] as between-chain SD varies from 8.4 to 21.0. Pooled mean: 62.4.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 62.4 /10 (weighted average of 4 chain means).
Shrinkage factor for chain C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=14.0: B = 0.994.
Interval [58.9, 59.0] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (58.9) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (62.4) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
